title: The Curse of Plenty: The Green Revolution and the Rise in Chronic Disease
notes: EVENT STUDY REVIEWED. Textbook event study — perfectly flat pre-trends, dramatic growing effect (0→80). All 3 estimators identical. Sekhri & Shastry (2024) AEJ:Applied. Table 3, Col 2. Stata: reghdfe hyv_major d_dmaq23 pr at, absorb(i.code i.year) vce(cluster code). 271 districts x 32 years (1956-1987). Treatment: thick/very-thick aquifer (dmaq23) x post-1966 Green Revolution. Single treatment timing (1966). 94 treated, 177 never-treated. Outcome: total HYV area (wheat+rice+maize+bajra+jowar). SA disabled (single treatment). Paper's main result is health (IHDS data not provided), this is the first-stage agricultural effect. | Outcome selection: Rule (ii) largest N. Health outcomes (Table 9 child height/weight) use restricted NFHS data not in replication package. Agricultural outcomes (HYV adoption) are the primary available outcome. Audit confirmed. | HonestDiD CS-NT infeasible: single treatment cohort (1966) with base_period='universal' (mandatory) causes all pre-treatment CS-DID ATTs to have SE=NA, making HonestDiD impossible. TWFE-only HonestDiD available.
